title: The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 41
summaries: "The History of Don Quixote, Volume 2, Part 41" by Miguel de Cervantes Saavedra is a section of a novel originally published in two parts in 1605 and 1615. This installment continues the adventures of Alonso Quijano, a minor nobleman who loses himself in chivalric romances and becomes the delusional knight-errant Don Quixote. Accompanied by his earthy squire Sancho Panza, Don Quixote mistakes windmills for giants and inns for castles, pursuing his fantastical quest to revive chivalry while reality and illusion increasingly collide. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: Spain -- Social life and customs -- 16th century -- Fiction
